title: Charmed Life - Chapter 8 (Fragment 3)
text:
Gwendolen looked after it critically. “Not bad,” she said. “I need one last touch though.”

Carefully, she dropped another tiny pinch of dragons’ blood on the flames. They burned with a whistling sound—brighter, sicker, yellower. Gwendolen began to chant again, waving her arms this time. After a moment, a shape seemed to be gathering in the quivering air over the flames. Whiteness was boiling, moving, forming into a miserable bent thing with a big head. Three more somethings were roiling and hardening beneath it. When the first thing flopped out of the flames onto the carpet, Gwendolen gave a gurgle of pleasure. Cat was amazed at how wicked she looked.

“Oh don’t!” he said. The three other somethings flopped onto the carpet too, and he saw they were the apparition at the window and three others like it. The first was like a baby that was too small to walk—except that it was walking, with its big head wobbling. The next was a cripple, so twisted and cramped upon itself that it could barely hobble. The third was the apparition at the window—pitiful, wrinkled, and draggled. The last had its white skin barred with blue stripes. All were weak and white and horrible. Cat shuddered all over.

“Please send them away!” he said.

Gwendolen only laughed again and waved the four apparitions towards the door.

They set off, toiling weakly. But they were only halfway there when Chrestomanci came through the door and Mr. Saunders came after him. In front of them came a shower of bones and small dead creatures, pattering onto the carpet and getting squashed under Chrestomanci’s long, shiny shoes. The apparitions hesitated, gibbering. Then they fled back to the flaming bowl and vanished. The flames vanished at the same time, into thick, black, smelly smoke.

Gwendolen stared at Chrestomanci and Mr. Saunders through the smoke. Chrestomanci was magnificent in dark blue velvet, with lace ruffles at his wrists and on the front of his shirt. Mr. Saunders seemed to have made an effort to find a suit that reached to the ends of his legs and arms, but had not quite succeeded. One of his big, black patent-leather boots was unlaced, and there was a lot of shirt and wrist showing as he slowly coiled an invisible skein of something around his bony right hand. Both he and Chrestomanci looked back at Gwendolen most unpleasantly.

“You were warned, you know,” Chrestomanci said. “Carry on, Michael.”

Mr. Saunders put the invisible skein in his pocket. “Thanks,” he said. “I’ve been itching to for a week now.” He strode down on Gwendolen in a billow of black coat, yanked her to her feet, hauled her to a chair, and put her facedown over his knee. There he dragged off his unlaced, big black boot and commenced spanking her with it hard and often.

While Mr. Saunders labored away, and Gwendolen screamed and squirmed and kicked, Chrestomanci marched up to Cat and boxed Cat’s ears, twice on each side. Cat was so surprised that he would have fallen over, had not Chrestomanci hit the other side of his head each time and brought him upright again.

vocabulary.definition: A specific ghostly figure appearing by the window.
vocabulary.example: The apparition at the window and three others like it.

vocabulary.definition: A ghostly figure or supernatural appearance.
vocabulary.example: He saw they were the apparition at the window and three others like it.

vocabulary.definition: A large undulating mass of something, typically cloud or smoke.
vocabulary.example: He strode down on Gwendolen in a billow of black coat.

vocabulary.definition: To hit someone on the ears, usually as a punishment or warning.
vocabulary.example: Chrestomanci boxed Cat's ears, twice on each side.

vocabulary.definition: To repeat or sing a phrase or song, often for ritualistic purposes.
vocabulary.example: Gwendolen began to chant again, waving her arms this time.

vocabulary.definition: To wind into continuous, regularly spaced rings one above the other.
vocabulary.example: He slowly coiled an invisible skein of something around his hand.

vocabulary.definition: In a way that expresses or involves an analysis of the merits and faults of something.
vocabulary.example: Gwendolen looked after it critically.

vocabulary.definition: Dirty and wet, typically of a person or thing.
vocabulary.example: The apparition at the window—pitiful, wrinkled, and draggled.

vocabulary.definition: To fall or drop heavily or awkwardly.
vocabulary.example: The first thing flopped out of the flames onto the carpet.

vocabulary.definition: To speak quickly and unintelligibly, typically out of fear or shock.
vocabulary.example: The apparitions hesitated, gibbering.

vocabulary.definition: Process of becoming hard or harder.
vocabulary.example: Three more somethings were roiling and hardening beneath it.

vocabulary.definition: To pull or drag forcibly.
vocabulary.example: He hauled her to a chair.

vocabulary.definition: To walk with difficulty, typically because of injury or weakness.
vocabulary.example: So twisted and cramped upon itself that it could barely hobble.

vocabulary.definition: To have a strong desire to do something.
vocabulary.example: I've been itching to for a week now.

vocabulary.definition: To work hard or exert oneself.
vocabulary.example: While Mr. Saunders labored away...

vocabulary.definition: Decorative frills made of lace often on clothing.
vocabulary.example: With lace ruffles at his wrists and on the front of his shirt.

vocabulary.definition: Impressively beautiful, elaborate, or extravagant.
vocabulary.example: Chrestomanci was magnificent.

vocabulary.definition: Very unhappy or unpleasant.
vocabulary.example: Forming into a miserable bent thing with a big head.

vocabulary.definition: Trembling or shaking with a slight rapid motion.
vocabulary.example: A shape seemed to be gathering in the quivering air over the flames.

vocabulary.definition: In a state of great turbulence or agitation, often used of liquids or emotions.
vocabulary.example: Three more somethings were roiling and hardening beneath it.

vocabulary.definition: To begin a journey or move out.
vocabulary.example: They set off, toiling weakly.

vocabulary.definition: To tremble convulsively, typically as a result of fear or revulsion.
vocabulary.example: Cat shuddered all over.

vocabulary.definition: A length of thread or yarn wound in a loose long coil.
vocabulary.example: He slowly coiled an invisible skein of something.

vocabulary.definition: To hit someone, especially a child, on their bottom as a punishment.
vocabulary.example: He commenced spanking her with his boot.

vocabulary.definition: To wriggle or twist the body from discomfort or embarrassment.
vocabulary.example: Gwendolen screamed and squirmed and kicked.

vocabulary.definition: To work extremely hard or incessantly.
vocabulary.example: They were only halfway there when... toiling weakly.

vocabulary.definition: To disappear suddenly and completely.
vocabulary.example: The flames vanished at the same time.

vocabulary.definition: In a very bad or deplorable state (synonymous to miserable in context).
vocabulary.example: Cat was amazed at how wicked she looked.
